title: Ceratochloa staminea
taxonomy: Ceratochloa staminea is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Chloridoideae and tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Ceratochloa is a part of the large grass family Poaceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Further, the classification of Ceratochloa staminea provides valuable information for taxonomists and researchers.

intro: Ceratochloa staminea, also known as the wild millet or green millet, is an annual grass native to various parts of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as aiding in digestion and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Ceratochloa staminea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction to make herbal teas, or as a dried powder to be used in capsules or supplements, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
